label: AC joint sprain with negative x-ray and no dislocation or neurovascular red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated AC joint sprain with x-ray performed and negative.
No fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, tendon or ligament rupture concern, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, neurovascular compromise, tendon or ligament rupture concern, severe or rapidly worsening pain, high-energy trauma, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: High-grade AC separation requiring separate planning.
Occult fracture or evolving emergency pain pattern.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports musculoskeletal strain, bruise, or overuse framing, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and escalation for infection, severe pain, worsening function, or neurovascular symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated musculoskeletal injury with fracture/dislocation, infection, neurovascular, pregnancy/pediatric, high-energy trauma, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis ac joint sprain xray negative
discharge diagnosis ac joint sprain xray negative
ac joint sprain xray negative diagnosed

ac joint sprain xray negative diagnosed
